description: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC social login with multi-provider identity brokering, account linking, profile normalization, PKCE/state/nonce CSRF protection, and configurable JWT or database session strategy.

actors.name: User
actors.description: Person initiating sign-in via an external identity provider

actors.name: OAuth / OIDC Provider
actors.description: External identity provider (e.g. Google, GitHub, Discord). Handles user authentication and returns an authorization code plus profile data.

actors.name: Auth Service
actors.description: Core authentication engine that orchestrates the OAuth flow — constructs authorization URLs, exchanges codes for tokens, normalises profiles, and manages account linking.

security.MUST: generate a cryptographically random state parameter for every authorization request; validate it on callback to prevent CSRF
support PKCE (RFC 7636) when provider.checks includes "pkce"; store code_verifier in an encrypted cookie and verify on callback
generate and validate a nonce for OIDC providers when provider.checks includes "nonce", to prevent ID token replay
store PKCE, state, and nonce values only in HTTP-only, encrypted cookies with SameSite=Lax; never expose them in URLs or response bodies
sanitise the callbackUrl redirect target; reject destinations outside the application's trusted origin to prevent open redirects

security.MUST NOT: automatically link a newly seen OAuth account to an existing user record by email address unless allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking is explicitly set to true for that provider
allow an OAuth account already linked to user A to be linked to user B; throw OAuthAccountNotLinked instead

security: SHOULD: require trustHost=true in reverse-proxy environments to ensure the redirect_uri is constructed from the correct host header
access: All endpoints under the /auth/* route prefix are public (no prior session required)
The signIn callback MAY block specific users by returning false; the result is surfaced as an AccessDenied error to the client

given: provider ID in callback path does not match any registered provider
configuration_error.result: Request redirected to error page with error=Configuration query parameter.

given: OAuth callback received

any: state cookie missing or does not match state query parameter
PKCE code_verifier cookie missing or code_challenge mismatch
OIDC nonce in ID token does not match stored nonce cookie

invalid_check.result: Redirect to error page; no session is created.

given: OAuth provider returned an error parameter in the callback URL
e.g. user denied consent, or provider server error
oauth_callback_error.result: Redirect to error page with provider error details (user-safe).

given: tokens successfully obtained from provider
provider.profile() throws or returns null/undefined
profile_parse_error.result: Redirect to error page; account not created.

given: profile normalised successfully
signIn callback returned false or a custom error URL
access_denied.result: Redirect to error page with error=AccessDenied.

given: OAuth account not previously seen
user is NOT currently signed in
provider.allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking is false or unset
a user record with the same email address already exists
email_conflict.result: Redirect to error page with error=OAuthAccountNotLinked. User must sign in with their original method first, then link the new provider from their profile settings.

given: OAuth account already exists in the accounts table
user IS currently signed in
the existing account belongs to a different user
provider_account_conflict.result: Redirect to error page with error=OAuthAccountNotLinked. The provider account cannot be reassigned across users.

given: user IS currently signed in (active session)
OAuth account not previously seen for this provider
signIn callback returned true
account_linked.result: New provider account linked to the currently signed-in user. Existing session updated; user redirected to callbackUrl.

given: OAuth account not previously seen
user is NOT currently signed in
no existing user with the same email address (or allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking: true)
signIn callback returned true
new_user_registered.result: New user record and account record created. Session issued (JWT cookie or database session token). User redirected to pages.newUser if configured, otherwise to callbackUrl.

given: OAuth account exists and is linked to a user
user is NOT currently signed in (or is signed in as the same user)
signIn callback returned true
sign_in_success.result: Session issued. JWT strategy: signed JWE stored in HTTP-only cookie (default maxAge 30 days). Database strategy: session record created, opaque token stored in cookie. User redirected to callbackUrl.

oauth_authorization_code_flow.description: Full OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE and state

steps.description: Generates state parameter (encrypted, 15-min TTL cookie), PKCE code_verifier + code_challenge if provider.checks includes "pkce", nonce if OIDC and provider.checks includes "nonce". Builds redirect to provider's authorization_endpoint with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, state, code_challenge.
Provider authenticates the user and shows a consent screen. On approval, redirects to redirect_uri with code and state.

steps.action: Validate callback and exchange code for tokens
steps.description: Validates state cookie matches state param (CSRF check). Validates PKCE code_verifier against stored challenge. POSTs to provider's token_endpoint with code and code_verifier. Receives access_token, refresh_token, id_token, expires_in.

OIDC: decodes claims from id_token (validated against JWKS). OAuth: requests userinfo endpoint with Bearer access_token. Calls provider.profile(rawProfile, tokens) to map provider-specific fields to standard {id, name, email, image} User shape.

Looks up accounts table by (provider, providerAccountId). If found → sign in as that user. If not found and session active → link to current user. If not found and email collision → error (unless allowDangerousEmailAccountLinking). If not found and no collision → create user + link account.

steps.action: Invoke callback chain and issue session
steps.description: Invokes signIn → redirect → jwt → session callbacks in order. Issues JWT (JWE, RS256) stored in __Secure-next-auth.session-token cookie, or creates database session record and issues opaque session token.
Browser receives session cookie and is redirected to callbackUrl (validated to be within the application's trusted origin).

errors.message: There is a problem with the server configuration. Check the server logs for more information.
To confirm your identity, sign in with the same account you used originally.
Try signing in with a different account.
Try signing in with a different account.
Try signing in with a different account.
Try signing in with a different account.

events.name: auth.sign_in
events.description: Fired after every successful sign-in, whether new user or returning. Includes whether the user is newly created.

events.name: auth.sign_out
events.description: Fired when a user signs out (JWT cookie deleted or database session revoked).

events.name: auth.create_user
events.description: Fired when a new user record is created in the database.

events.name: auth.update_user
events.description: Fired when an existing user record is updated.

events.name: auth.link_account
events.description: Fired when a new OAuth account is linked to a user (both new registrations and explicit account linking while signed in).

events.name: auth.configuration_error
events.description: Auth system misconfiguration detected (e.g. unknown provider).

events.name: auth.invalid_check
events.description: CSRF/replay protection check failed during OAuth callback.

events.name: auth.account_not_linked
events.description: Account linking blocked — email conflict or account already owned by another user.

events.name: auth.oauth_callback_error
events.description: OAuth provider returned an error in the callback redirect.

events.name: auth.access_denied
events.description: signIn callback blocked the user from signing in.

related.reason: OIDC client lifecycle and JWKS management
Federated enterprise identity alongside social providers

goals.description: OAuth 2.0 / OIDC social login with multi-provider identity brokering, account linking, and session management

success_metrics.measurement: Successful sign-ins divided by total sign-in attempts
Errors that resolve without manual intervention

constraints.description: PKCE and state validation must never be skipped
Must degrade gracefully when a provider is unavailable

tradeoffs.reason: Auto-linking accounts by email without provider trust verification creates account-takeover attack surfaces

session_options: database (requires adapter — Prisma, MongoDB, Drizzle, etc.)
